1.text: Choose the statement that best describes the photograph.

choices.A: Attendance at the event is higher than it normally is.
choices.B: Several people are focusing on the man standing up.
choices.C: The professor is lecturing to the students in his class.
choices.D: Notes are being taken about the lessons being learned.

2.text: Choose the statement that best describes the photograph.

choices.A: Several passengers are getting on the bus.
choices.B: Pedestrians are crossing the street at the crosswalk.
choices.C: The sprinters are having a race downtown.
choices.D: Some cyclists are riding in a line on the street.

3.text: Choose the statement that best describes the photograph.

choices.A: The chef is preparing meals for all of the customers.
choices.B: Plates of food have been placed in front of the diners.
choices.C: All of the seats at the restaurant are currently occupied.
choices.D: A waiter is taking an order from one of the customers.

4.text: Choose the statement that best describes the photograph.

choices.A: Shoppers are heading up the escalator.
choices.B: Some people are waiting to get on the elevator.
choices.C: Everyone is heading in the same direction.
choices.D: Both sides of the escalator are currently in use.

5.text: Choose the statement that best describes the photograph.

choices.A: Merchandise has been stocked on several shelves.
choices.B: The customer is putting items in the grocery cart.
choices.C: The prices of some of the items have been reduced.
choices.D: Fresh produce is being placed in the refrigerators.

6.text: Choose the statement that best describes the photograph.

choices.A: There are paintings on all of the room's walls.
choices.B: Cushions are being placed on the sofa.
choices.C: A table is in between the couch and the television.
choices.D: One of the doors in the room has been opened.

7.text: The files have all been submitted, haven't they?

choices.C: Mr. Peters wants to see them.

8.text: I recommend applying for a job at Ermine Consulting.

choices.B: That's what I'm planning to do.

9.text: How long do you intend to wait for Mr. Schnell?

10.text: What time is the flight supposed to land?

11.text: Shouldn't you submit the application form at once?

choices.C: Yes, he has very good form.

12.text: Do you happen to know who the head accountant is?

choices.A: He works in the Accounting Department.

18.text: Why don't we meet Mr. Richardson at the restaurant after work?

20.text: What's your opinion of that article on the front page?

30.text: Do you mind taking a look at this budget form?

choices.A: We went over budget last month.
choices.B: Yes, we are minding our own business.
choices.C: Can I do that after the conference?

105.text: Ms. Hampton decided to take her vacation in the first week of August _____ on a trip abroad with her family.

choices.A: To request that some meetings be scheduled
choices.C: To thank a person for the work she did
choices.D: To apologize for a heavy workload

192.text: What is suggested about the Rosebud at 494 Sedona Avenue?

choices.A: It was the first Rosebud ever to open.
choices.C: A Pennington's is located near it.
choices.D: The store is not attracting customers.
